Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make daily digest collapsible (#5)

* Track and display exact lift opening times

- Add first_opened_at column to daily_summary table with migration for existing DBs
- Record timestamp when a lift is first seen as "open" each day
- Display opening time in status pill (e.g. "Opened 9:30 AM") instead of just "Opened"
- Backfill existing opening times from terrain_snapshots on startup
- Add /api/last-scrape endpoint to diagnose missing scrape data
- Pass scraped_at through all update_daily_summary call sites

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Fix resort cards not showing and add snapshot backfill for missing dates

Root cause: /api/status only returned resorts with daily_summary data.
When the scraper failed, no terrain rows existed, so ALL resort cards
disappeared — including weather that was working independently.

Fixes:
- Always return all 4 tracked resorts in /api/status even without terrain data
- Add try/except to run_scrape() with per-resort error logging
- Add backfill_daily_from_snapshots() to recover daily_summary from
  terrain_snapshots when the save pipeline crashed partway through
- Auto-backfill last 7 days of snapshot data on startup
- Add POST /api/backfill endpoint for manual date recovery

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Add Powder Mountain (James Peak), 6am scraper start, smart digest triggering

- Add Powder Mountain scraper tracking James Peak terrain via Playwright
- Change terrain scrape schedule from 7am to 6am MT start
- Replace fixed digest cron with smart triggering: auto-generates after
  both avalanche forecast and terrain data are available for the day
- Keep 10am fallback digest in case auto-trigger doesn't fire
- Add Powder Mountain to weather forecasts, digest, and frontend
- Keep avalanche schedule at 5-9am as requested

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Make daily digest collapsible — starts minimized, click to expand

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

commit.message:
Enhance tmux-sessionizer with GitHub repo creation and pane setup (#4)

* feat: add claude-code install and update tmux-sessionizer layout

- Add claude-code to brew packages in config.ini so it is installed by run.sh
- Scope tmux-sessionizer directory selection to $HOME/Code subdirectories only
- On new session creation, auto-build a three-pane layout:
    left (75%): nvim
    top-right (25% wide, 62% tall): claude
    bottom-right (25% wide, 38% tall): shell
- Handle the case where tmux is running but the user is not inside it

https://claude.ai/code/session_01L3ikA4vECEC92ApwrNqD9S

* fix: use brew install --cask for claude-code

Add a brew_cask installer type to config.ini so claude-code is installed
via `brew install --cask` rather than `brew install`. Update run.sh to
apply the macOS sudo prefix for brew_cask packages the same way it does
for brew packages.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01L3ikA4vECEC92ApwrNqD9S

* fix: replace fixed-depth find with marker-based project discovery

Use .git, go.mod, Cargo.toml, package.json, and pyproject.toml as root
markers with -prune so find searches at any depth without a hardcoded maxdepth.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01L3ikA4vECEC92ApwrNqD9S

* fix: list direct Code children + github.com grandchildren for session picker

Replaces marker-based find with explicit directory listing:
- All direct subdirs of $HOME/Code
- All grandchild dirs of $HOME/Code/github.com (org/repo layout)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01L3ikA4vECEC92ApwrNqD9S

* fix: exclude github.com folder itself from session picker list

https://claude.ai/code/session_01L3ikA4vECEC92ApwrNqD9S

* feat: allow creating a new GitHub repo from fzf session picker

When the user types a name with no matching dir and presses Enter, the
script creates a private GitHub repo via `gh repo create`, clones it into
$HOME/Code/github.com/<user>/<name>, and opens it as a new tmux session.

Uses fzf --print-query to distinguish between "selected existing dir" (exit 0,
line 2 non-empty) and "typed new name" (exit 1, line 2 empty).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01L3ikA4vECEC92ApwrNqD9S

* fix: ensure $HOME/Code/github.com exists before searching or cloning

mkdir -p guarantees the directory is present, removing the need for the
conditional check around the github.com find call.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01L3ikA4vECEC92ApwrNqD9S

* style: increase left pane width from 75% to 90%

https://claude.ai/code/session_01L3ikA4vECEC92ApwrNqD9S

* fix: replace deprecated -p flag with -l in split-window calls

-p was deprecated in tmux 3.1+ and replaced by -l with a % suffix.
Use -l 10% and -l 38% instead of -p 10 and -p 38.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01L3ikA4vECEC92ApwrNqD9S

* fix: seed detached session size and set right pane to 25%

new-session -d creates sessions at a tiny default size, causing
percentage-based splits to be calculated incorrectly before attach.
Pass -x/-y from tput so the session size matches the real terminal.

Also update right-pane width from 10% to 25%.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01L3ikA4vECEC92ApwrNqD9S

* feat: include children of Code/playground in sessionizer search

Expand playground like github.com — exclude it from direct children
and instead include its children individually in the fzf list.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01L3ikA4vECEC92ApwrNqD9S

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf: increase micro/GTX1080Ti GPU utilization

- seq_len range: [5,8,10,15,20] → [10,15,20,30,40,50] (longer sequences = more FLOPS/batch)
- hidden sizes: max 512 → 1024 for most archs (4x FLOPS per batch at h=1024)
  - VRAM safe: model 33MB + activations 200MB × 2 workers ≈ 466MB on 11GB GPU

Both changes increase compute per batch without adding data, improving GPU saturation
from ~34% toward 60-80% on GTX 1080 Ti with H1 dataset.

commit.message:
Fix matchmaking: use max tolerance of both players + polling fallback

Three fixes for players not matching:

1. Bidirectional tolerance: now uses Math.max(myTolerance, oppTolerance).
   If Player1 waited 14s (tolerance 70%) and Player2 waited 2s (tolerance
   10%), they match because Player1's wider range covers both. Each pool
   entry stores enteredAt (client timestamp) so both sides can calculate
   each other's tolerance.

2. Polling fallback: if onSnapshot fails (e.g. Firestore security rules
   block collection listeners), a setInterval polling loop kicks in every
   2.5s as a backup. The snapshotWorking flag prevents duplicate work.

3. Extracted tryMatchOpponents helper: shared by both onSnapshot and
   polling paths, ensuring identical matching logic.

commit.message:
fix: prevent auto-start timeout by skipping step sequence generation

The first AI message was timing out on Vercel because generateStepSequence
generates 3-5 diagrams sequentially (30-150s). Now skips step sequence for
auto-start; single engine diagram (parallel) still works. Also adds client-side
AbortController timeout and retry logic for auto-start failures.

commit.message:
Fix multi-instance session split-brain — lock to 1 CF instance

With 2 instances, session files uploaded to one container were invisible
to the other, causing intermittent 'no session found' errors. Set
instances: 1 in manifest.yml to prevent this on future cf push.

commit.message:
feat: add Snowflake data ingestion endpoint for Workato

Add /api/workato/ingest-snowflake-data endpoint to handle large data
imports (500k+ records) from Snowflake via Workato.

Features:
- Accepts JSON array of records
- Batch processing (1000 records per batch) for efficiency
- Stores data in flexible JSONB format
- Progress logging for large imports
- Indexed by data_type and ingestion timestamp

Database:
- New table: snowflake_data (id, data_type, record_data, ingested_at)
- JSONB column for flexible schema support
- Indexes for query performance

Usage:
POST /api/workato/ingest-snowflake-data
{
  "data_type": "churn",
  "records": [{ ...snowflake fields... }]
}

Ready for customization based on actual Snowflake schema.

commit.message:
style: Fix not operator spacing per Pint rules

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix PDF export missing deletions/modifications (shared extraction utility)

Root cause: _build_doc_from_pdf used get_text("blocks") with line-by-line
splitting while PdfParser used get_text("dict") with block-level joining
after the P1 fix. This mismatch caused _match_para_index to fail to find
anchor paragraphs for deletions and modifications (ratio fell below 0.8),
so only additions were rendered in the exported DOCX.

P2: Extract _normalize_pdf_text() and _pdf_extract_blocks() as shared
module-level functions in parser.py. PdfParser.parse() now delegates
extraction to _pdf_extract_blocks() and its _extract_paragraphs() is
renamed _classify_paragraphs() to reflect its narrower role.
_build_doc_from_pdf() in output_generator.py now calls _pdf_extract_blocks()
so both paths always produce identical paragraph text.

P3: _match_para_index() gains a threshold parameter (default 0.8).
generate_redline_docx() passes threshold=0.65 for PDF originals as a
safety net against minor encoding differences that survive normalisation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: replace flat memory stream with three-tier memory architecture

Tier 0 (Core Memory): identity, emotional state, player summary, NPC
summaries, active goals, key facts — always included in every Gemini
call. Player summary auto-updated by LLM after conversations.

Tier 1 (Episodic Memory): no hard cap, stability-based decay per type,
deduplication via hash + Jaccard similarity, state-change detection marks
old observations as superseded. Eliminates ~64 duplicate env memories
per NPC per day.

Tier 2 (Archival Summaries): empty structure for future compression.

Hybrid retrieval: 0.5*relevance + 0.3*recency + 0.2*importance with
power-law decay, testing effect (+10% stability on recall).

Batch embedding API via batchEmbedContents, per-NPC embedding queue
processing 10 memories every 5 seconds.

Fully backward-compatible with existing save format.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(iam.presentation): implement POST /workspaces endpoint with TDD

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(shared_kernel): align SpiceDB schema with translator implementation

Fix workspace definition in schema.zed and ConfigMap to match the
relationships actually created by the IAM outbox translator.

Schema changes (workspace definition):
- Add `relation tenant: tenant` for organizational ownership
- Change `relation parent: tenant` to `relation parent: workspace` for hierarchy
- Rename `owner` to `admin` for consistency with tenant/group definitions
- Rename `permission delete` to `permission manage` for consistency
- Add Phase 3 comments for member/permission usage

ConfigMap changes (full sync with schema.zed):
- Apply all workspace definition fixes above
- Add missing `relation member: user` to tenant definition
- Fix tenant `permission view = admin` to `permission view = admin + member`
- Add missing `permission administrate = admin` to tenant definition
- Add missing `api_key` definition (was in schema.zed but not ConfigMap)
- Add future resource type comments

Inconsistencies found and documented:
1. Schema had `relation parent: tenant` but translator writes
   `workspace#tenant@tenant` (relation name 'tenant') and
   `workspace#parent@workspace` (parent type 'workspace')
2. ConfigMap was missing tenant `member` relation, `administrate`
   permission, and entire `api_key` definition
3. RelationType.WORKSPACE enum exists but is unused by any translator
4. Permission.DELETE enum value corresponds to removed `permission delete`
   in workspace; may need cleanup in Phase 3
5. Schema `owner` relation on workspace renamed to `admin` to align with
   tenant and group naming conventions

All 970 unit tests pass (3 pre-existing SSL failures unrelated).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(shared_kernel): remove unused authorization enum values

Remove RelationType.WORKSPACE and Permission.DELETE which have no usage
in the codebase. Neither value corresponds to any relation or permission
in the current SpiceDB schema. They can be re-added when future resource
types (knowledge_graph, data_source) are implemented.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(shared_kernel): update future schema references to use admin relation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(api): add Management architecture boundary tests (AIHCM-187)

Define pytest-archon rules enforcing DDD layer boundaries and cross-context
isolation for the Management bounded context, plus comprehensive cross-context
isolation for Graph and Query contexts, and outbox infrastructure composition rules.

- Management DDD layer rules (skipif subpackages don't exist yet)
- Management cross-context isolation (management must not import iam/graph/etc)
- Graph cross-context isolation (comprehensive, per-layer)
- Query cross-context isolation (comprehensive, per-layer)
- Outbox infrastructure must not import bounded context internals
- Shared kernel rule for management already existed (no-op)
- Management __init__.py stub to satisfy pytest-archon .check()

* chore: lockfile

* fix(api): harden _subpackage_exists and add ingestion/extraction outbox rules

- _subpackage_exists now only returns False when the target package
  itself is missing; re-raises ModuleNotFoundError for broken nested
  imports so real errors surface immediately.
- Add outbox isolation tests for ingestion and extraction contexts.

* fix(api): add ports and presentation to outbox isolation rules

The outbox should_not_import rules only blocked domain, application,
and infrastructure layers, leaving ports and presentation uncovered.
Added both to all six bounded context outbox isolation tests.

Default timeout reduced to 5s, extracted checkOnce helper for cleaner
polling loop, re-reads partner from DB each cycle. SKILL.md updated
with comprehensive coord_wait_for_reply documentation.

feat(build): replace /build Claude prompt with build.sh Bash script

Solves context exhaustion (22.5% after 2/7 slices) and 3-level nesting
limitation. Each phase (Planner, Orchestrate) now runs in its own
`claude -p` session with 100% fresh context. State passed via files.

Add Live page for in-progress tournaments

- New /live route shows current picks for any tournament with
  status = 'in_progress', with a pulsing green indicator
- Earnings column hidden until results are in, with a note at the bottom
- 🟢 Live tab appears in nav only when a tournament is in progress
- Countdown banner now only shows for 'upcoming' tournaments (not in-progress)
- Graceful empty state when no tournament is active

Recognize returning members in registration form

On email blur, check /api/account/check-email — if a matching account
exists, pre-fill name and phone, skip the community agreements (already
agreed), and show a warm welcome-back note. Never blocks the flow:
form works normally if check fails or user submits before it completes.
